name: Metformin blunted some aerobic exercise adaptations in older adults
summary: An older-adult exercise trial is an important functional and mitochondrial-adaptation boundary for metformin and exercise-mimetic claims.
statement: In older adults randomized during 12 weeks of aerobic exercise training, metformin attenuated exercise-induced gains in whole-body insulin sensitivity and VO2 max and abrogated the exercise-mediated increase in skeletal-muscle mitochondrial respiration, with variable positive and negative responder patterns.
population_or_model: Older adults undergoing aerobic exercise training
quantitative_note: The PubMed abstract reports placebo n=26 and metformin n=27.
caveats: The trial tested adjunctive metformin during exercise, not metformin monotherapy.
Exercise training improved several measures regardless of treatment, so the result is a context-specific interaction.
The finding argues against broad preventive use without phenotype and co-intervention stratification.
